6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechAcyclic
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
9	
10	/-!
11	# Sub-brick A: identifying the evaluated augmented Čech section complex
12	  (blueprint `lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma` … `lem:cechSection_contractible`)
13	
14	This file is the shared "Sub-brick A" chain that
15	
16	1. identifies the degree-`p` Čech-nerve backbone `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj [p]` with
17	   the coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` in `Over X` (`cechBackbone_left_sigma`);
18	2. decomposes the push-pull object `pushPullObj F Y_p` as a product in `X.Modules`
19	   (`pushPull_sigma_iso`) — the single new-infra leaf;
20	3. identifies the sections of each leg over an open `V` with `Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
21	   (`pushPull_leg_sections`);
22	4. assembles the degreewise section isomorphism `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
23	   (`pushPull_eval_prod_iso`);
24	5. promotes these degreewise isos to a complex isomorphism `D ≅ D'`
25	   (`cechSection_complex_iso`); and
26	6. produces the contracting homotopy `Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0` on the concrete complex whenever
27	   `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix` (`cechSection_contractible`).
28	
29	The result is consumed by `CechAugmentedResolution.lean` to close the residual `hSec`.
30	
31	Blueprint: §Sub-brick A decomposition, `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`,
32	lemmas `lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma` through `lem:cechSection_contractible`.
33	-/
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits Scheme.Modules Opposite
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
42	
43	/-! ## Stub 1 — Geometric backbone identification -/
44	
45	/- Planner strategy:
46	Goal: `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p]) ≅ ∐ fun σ => Over.mk (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).ι`
47	in `Over X`.
48	
49	Route:
50	(a) UNPACK `coverCechNerveOver`: it is `Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom`,
51	    so the degree-`p` object is `Over.mk ((coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom.app (mk p))`.
52	    The underlying scheme is `(coverCechNerve 𝒰).left.obj (op (mk p))` — the `(p+1)`-fold
53	    fibre power of `coverArrow 𝒰 = Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` over `X`.
54	
55	(b) DISTRIBUTE: coproducts distribute over finite fibre products in `Scheme`
56	    (`Sigma.fiberProduct_sigma` or similar Mathlib anchor):
57	    `(∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ×_X ⋯ ×_X (∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ≅ ∐_σ (U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p})`
58	    for `σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`.
59	
60	(c) INTERSECT: each factor `U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p}` is the scheme-level intersection
61	    (fibre product of open immersions over `X`), which is the open subscheme
62	    `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` with structure map `(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).ι`.
63	
64	(d) IDENTIFY: the structure map of the `σ`-component is the open immersion `j_σ`, and the
65	    universal map out of the coproduct is `Sigma.desc (fun σ => j_σ)`, making the LHS
66	    equal to `∐_σ Over.mk (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).ι` as an `Over X` object.
67	
68	Key Mathlib anchors:
69	- `Scheme.pullback_openCover_iSup` or sigma-fibre-product distribution in `Scheme`
70	- `Scheme.IsOpenImmersion.isPullback` (open immersions are pullback-stable)
71	- `ColimitCocone` machinery for the coproduct in `Over X`
72	
73	Difficulty: MEDIUM — geometric bookkeeping, not sheaf theory. -/
74	noncomputable def cechBackbone_left_sigma (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
75	    (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≅
76	    ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
77	      Over.mk (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).ι :=
78	  sorry
79	
80	/-! ## Stub 2 — Push-pull over the Čech backbone is the product over intersection opens -/
81	
82	/- Planner strategy:
83	Goal: `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).ι)` in `X.Modules`.
84	where `Y_p = (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op (mk p))`.
85	
86	This is THE new-infra leaf. The key observation is that, although the opens `U_σ ⊆ X` OVERLAP
87	inside `X`, they are DISJOINT as components of the coproduct scheme `Y_p = ∐_σ U_σ`.
88	
89	Route:
90	(a) TRANSPORT via `cechBackbone_left_sigma`: by the iso from Stub 1, we may work with the
91	    coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` instead of `Y_p`.
92	
93	(b) BUILD comparison map:
94	    `pushPullObj F Y_p ⟶ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`
95	    from the projections `pushPullMap F (ι_σ) : pushPullObj F Y_p ⟶ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`
96	    induced by the coproduct inclusions `ι_σ : Over.mk j_σ ⟶ Y_p` (going backwards via
97	    the pushPullFunctor, which is contravariant on `Over X`).
98	
99	(c) CHECK iso via `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`:
100	    The forgetful functor `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf = SheafOfModules.forget ⋙
101	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf ...` is faithful, reflects isos, and preserves limits
102	    (`Sheaf.lean:75–78`). So it suffices to verify the comparison is an iso at the
103	    `Ab`-presheaf level.
104	
105	(d) PRESHEAF-LEVEL ISO: on `Ab`-presheaves, this is the indexed disjoint-union decomposition.
106	    Since the components of `∐_σ U_σ` are disjoint in the coproduct topology:
107	    * Iterate the binary `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` (Lean name: same) over the
108	      finite index set `{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀}`.
109	    * Or use `Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso` (sections over a binary coproduct scheme = product)
110	      as the binary building block and iterate.
111	    The result: for any open `W` in `Y_p`, `(q_p^* F).val.obj (op W) ≅ ∏_σ (j_σ^* F).val.obj (op (W_σ))`
112	    where `W_σ = (ι_σ)⁻¹W` is the trace on the σ-component.
113	
114	(e) TRANSPORT back through `toPresheaf` to get the iso in `X.Modules`.
115	
116	Key Mathlib anchors:
117	- `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` (Topology/Sheaves/SheafCondition/PairwiseIntersections.lean)
118	- `Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso` (AlgebraicGeometry/Limits.lean)
119	- `SheafOfModules.forget` faithfulness and iso-reflection (`Sheaf.lean:75–78`)
120	
121	Difficulty: HARD (genuine new sheaf infra — the single new-infra leaf of the chain). -/
122	noncomputable def pushPull_sigma_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
123	    (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ) :
124	    pushPullObj F ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))) ≅
125	    ∏ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
126	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).ι) :=
127	  sorry
128	
129	/-! ## Stub 3 — Per-leg section identification -/
130	
131	/- Planner strategy:
132	Goal: `Γ(V, pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)) ≅ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` as `Ab` objects,
133	where `j_σ = (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).ι : (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme ⟶ X`.
134	
135	Three off-the-shelf identifications, chained:
136	
137	(1) PUSHFORWARD SECTIONS = PREIMAGE SECTIONS (`pushforward_obj_obj`, `rfl`, Sheaf.lean:155):
138	    `Γ(V, (j_σ)_* N) = Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, N)` for any `N : (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme.Modules`.
139	    Apply to `N = (j_σ)^* F = Scheme.Modules.pullback j_σ |>.obj F`.
140	
141	(2) PULLBACK ALONG OPEN IMMERSION = RESTRICTION (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Sheaf.lean:371):
142	    `(j_σ)^* F ≅ F.restrict j_σ` as `(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme.Modules` objects.
143	    This is already used in `QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean:113–114,248`.
144	
145	(3) SECTIONS OF RESTRICTION = SECTIONS OF IMAGE-PREIMAGE (`restrict_obj`, `rfl`, Sheaf.lean:328):
146	    `Γ(W, F.restrict j_σ) = Γ(j_σ ''ᵁ W, F)` for any `W` in the source scheme.
147	    Applied to `W = j_σ⁻¹V`: `j_σ ''ᵁ (j_σ⁻¹V) = U_σ ∩ V` (since `j_σ` is an open
148	    immersion: image-of-preimage = intersection with image = `U_σ ∩ V`).
149	
150	Compose (1)+(2)+(3): `Γ(V, (j_σ)_*(j_σ)^*F) = Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, (j_σ)^*F) ≅ Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, F.restrict j_σ)
151	= Γ(j_σ ''ᵁ (j_σ⁻¹V), F) = Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`.
152	
153	Key Lean names:
154	- `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj` (rfl)
155	- `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
156	- `Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj` (rfl)
157	- `Opens.image_preimage` or `IsOpenImmersion.image_preimage_eq_inf` for the final equality
158	
159	Difficulty: LOW (three off-the-shelf steps, two of them rfl). -/
160	noncomputable def pushPull_leg_sections (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
161	    (F : X.Modules) {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
162	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
163	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
164	          (pushPullObj F (Over.mk (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).ι))).presheaf.obj
165	        (Opposite.op V) ≅
166	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
167	        (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V)) :=
168	  sorry

170	/-! ## Stub 4 — Degreewise section identification of the Čech backbone -/
171	
172	/- Planner strategy:
173	Goal: `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` as `Ab` objects.
174	
175	Assemble three pieces in sequence:
176	
177	(A) PRODUCT DECOMPOSITION (`pushPull_sigma_iso`, Stub 2):
178	    `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`.
179	
180	(B) EVALUATION PRESERVES PRODUCTS (`SheafOfModules.evaluationPreservesLimitsOfShape`,
181	    `Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Limits.lean:85`):
182	    `Γ(V, ∏_σ N_σ) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(V, N_σ)`.
183	    Applied here: `Γ(V, ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(V, pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ))`.
184	
185	(C) PER-LEG IDENTIFICATION (`pushPull_leg_sections`, Stub 3):
186	    `Γ(V, pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)) ≅ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` for each σ.
187	
188	Chain (A)+(B)+(C) using natural isomorphisms + pointwise composition.
189	
190	Key Lean names:
191	- `pushPull_sigma_iso` (Stub 2)
192	- `SheafOfModules.evaluationPreservesLimitsOfShape` (or `preservesLimitsOfShape_evaluation`)
193	- `pushPull_leg_sections` (Stub 3)
194	- `Functor.mapIso` to apply the evaluation functor to the iso from (A)
195	
196	Difficulty: LOW (assembly of Stubs 2 and 3 plus an off-the-shelf limits lemma). -/
197	noncomputable def pushPull_eval_prod_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
198	    (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
199	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
200	          (pushPullObj F
201	            ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))))).presheaf.obj
202	        (Opposite.op V) ≅
203	    ∏ᶜ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
204	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
205	        (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V)) :=
206	  sorry

208	/-! ## Stub 5 — Complex-level iso: evaluated augmented Čech section complex ≅ concrete complex -/
209	
210	/- Planner strategy:
211	Goal: `D ≅ D'` as `CochainComplex AddCommGrpCat ℕ`, where
212	  - `D = (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj Kp` is the evaluated augmented Čech section complex
213	    (GV = `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf ⋙ evaluation(op V)`,
214	     Kp = `(SheafOfModules.forget ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 ·)).mapHC.obj K`,
215	     K = `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F`); and
216	  - `D' = sectionCechComplex (fun i => coverOpen 𝒰 i ⊓ V) Fp` is the concrete section Čech
217	    complex (with `Fp = (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F`).
218	
219	Route (promote degreewise isos to a complex iso):
220	
221	(A) DEGREEWISE OBJECT ISO: `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4) gives, for each `p`,
222	    `D.X p ≅ D'.X p` as `Ab` objects — both equal `∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`.
223	
224	(B) DIFFERENTIAL MATCH: The differential of `D'` is, read through `sectionCechProductEquiv`
225	    (`CechAcyclic.lean:1438`), the alternating sum `∑_i (-1)^i • sectionCechFaceRestr(σ,i)`
226	    (`sectionCech_objD_apply`, `CechAcyclic.lean:1513`). The differential of `D` is the
227	    evaluation-at-`V` of the Čech-nerve coface maps; under the degreewise identification
228	    (A), each coface of `D` becomes the corresponding face restriction of `D'`. REUSE
229	    `sectionCech_objD_apply` rather than rebuilding the alternating-sum bookkeeping.
230	
231	(C) ASSEMBLE: Build the `HomologicalComplex.mkIso` (or `HomologicalComplex.Hom` iso) from
232	    the degreewise components, checking the `comm` condition via the differential match.
233	
234	AMBIGUITY FLAG: The type of `Kp` in the definition of `D` uses
235	`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)` as a technical adapter between
236	`SheafOfModules.forget` landing in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.val` and the
237	`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj` that the evaluation uses. The prover
238	should verify this adapter type carefully; if the exact path differs from the scaffold,
239	adjust `Kp` accordingly. Checking how `hSec` in `CechAugmentedResolution.lean:185-205`
240	constructs `Kp` provides the canonical reference.
241	
242	Key Lean names:
243	- `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4)
244	- `sectionCech_objD_apply` (CechAcyclic.lean:1513)
245	- `sectionCechProductEquiv` (CechAcyclic.lean:1438)
246	- `HomologicalComplex.mkIso` or `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`
247	
248	Difficulty: MEDIUM (assembly + differential bookkeeping via sectionCech_objD_apply). -/
249	noncomputable def cechSection_complex_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]

/-- **Transport `IsZero` of homology across a complex iso plus a null-homotopic identity.**
If `D ≅ D'` as homological complexes and the identity of `D'` is homotopic to `0`, then
`D.homology p` is a zero object.  This is the consumer-side glue for the section-{\v C}ech
route: the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech complex `D` is identified with a concrete section
complex `D'` (`cechSection_complex_iso`) which carries a contracting homotopy
(`cechSection_contractible`); this lemma turns that pair into `IsZero (D.homology p)`.
Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological algebra). -/
lemma isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
    [CategoryWithHomology C] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι}
    {D D' : HomologicalComplex C c} (e : D ≅ D') (p : ι)
    (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) :=
  IsZero.of_iso (isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero D' p ho)
    ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor C c p).mapIso e)

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex

This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.

We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**

The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.

Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj

/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange

/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.

For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)

/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)

/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
  subst e; simp

/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**

The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.

Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).

Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
Mathlib counterpart. -/
noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))

Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean

```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

10	/-!
11	# Higher direct images along open immersions of affine opens
12	
13	This file establishes `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`:
14	
15	1. `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic`: for an open immersion `j : U ↪ X` of an
16	   affine open `U` into a separated scheme `X`, the higher direct images `R^q j_* H` vanish
17	   for `q ≥ 1` and any quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H`.
18	
19	2. `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp` (pinned declaration): consequently, for any
20	   morphism `f : X ⟶ S` and quasi-coherent `H` on `U`, there is a canonical isomorphism
21	   `R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ R^k (f ∘ j)_* H`.
22	
23	Blueprint: `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`.
24	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`.
25	-/
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits
30	
31	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/
32	
33	namespace CategoryTheory.Functor
34	
35	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
36	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then `X`
37	is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` + `Functor.map_injective`.)
38	
39	Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory).  A copy of the same lemma in
40	`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`; reproduced here because that sibling file is not in this
41	file's import chain. -/
42	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
43	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms D]
44	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
45	    (h : Limits.IsZero (F.obj Y)) : Limits.IsZero Y := by
46	  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
47	  apply F.map_injective
48	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
49	  exact h
50	
51	end CategoryTheory.Functor
52	
53	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a sectionwise-locally-zero presheaf -/
54	
55	namespace CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology
56	
57	open Opposite in
58	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf whose sections are locally zero vanishes.**
59	If for every object `U` and every section `s : Q(U)` there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U`
60	on which `s` restricts to `0`, then the sheafification of `Q` is a zero object.
61	
62	This is the *sectionwise* (local-injectivity) strengthening of
63	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero`: that lemma requires the *objectwise* vanishing
64	`Q.obj (op V) = 0` for every member `V` of a single covering sieve, which forces the
65	vanishing to hold on a downward-closed family.  Here only the restricted *section* need
66	vanish, so the witnessing sieve can be generated by a cofinal *basis* of "good" opens
67	(e.g. the affine opens, where the section group vanishes) even though the basis itself is
68	not downward closed.  The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero
69	presheaf) is read off from the hypothesis applied to `x - y`; local surjectivity is free.
70	Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
71	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero {C : Type*} [Category C]
72	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
73	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
74	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
75	    (h : ∀ (U : C) (s : ToType (Q.obj (op U))), ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
76	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) s = 0) :
77	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by
78	  let Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
79	  have hZobj : ∀ x : Cᵒᵖ, IsZero (Z.obj x) := by
80	    intro x
81	    change IsZero (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
82	    exact AddCommGrpCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
83	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
84	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
85	    refine ⟨?_⟩
86	    intro X x y _
87	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop (x - y)
88	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
89	    intro V g hg
90	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y
91	    have h0 : (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) (x - y) = 0 := hSzero hg
92	    rw [map_sub] at h0
93	    exact sub_eq_zero.mp h0
94	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
95	    refine ⟨?_⟩
96	    intro U s
97	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
98	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
99	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
100	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
101	  exact isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ
102	
103	end CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology
104	
105	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
106	
107	open Scheme.Modules
108	
109	variable {U X S : Scheme.{u}}
110	
111	/- Planner strategy:
112	Part (1): `R^q j_* H` is the sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)` (presheaf description
113	`higher_direct_image_presheaf`, Stacks 01XJ); for affine `V`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine (U affine,
114	X separated), so `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `H^q` for `q ≥ 1`; affine opens are a basis ⇒
115	`R^q j_* H = 0`. Part (2): take an injective resolution `H → I•`; `j_* I•` is a resolution of
116	`j_* H` (each `j_* Iⁿ` is `j_*`-acyclic by (1)) and each `j_* Iⁿ` is `f_*`-acyclic (same presheaf
117	+ Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); so `j_* I•` is an `f_*`-acyclic resolution of `j_* H`,
118	and the P4 acyclic-resolution comparison (`acyclic_resolution_computes_derived` /
119	`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) with `G = f_*` gives `R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ H^k(f_*(j_* I•)) =
120	H^k((f∘j)_* I•) = R^k (f∘j)_* H`, using `f_* ∘ j_* = (f∘j)_*`. Blueprint:
121	`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`. Source: Stacks `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`.
122	-/
123	
124	/-- **Higher direct images of an affine open immersion vanish** (part (1) of blueprint
125	`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`; Stacks `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
126	
127	For an open immersion `j : U ↪ X` where `U` is affine and `X` is separated, the morphism
128	`j` is affine (since for any affine open `V ⊆ X`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine by separatedness).
129	Therefore `R^q j_* H = 0` for every `q ≥ 1` and every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H`.
130	
131	Proof route: by `higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology`, `R^q j_* H` is the
132	sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)`. For affine `V`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine (`U` affine
133	+ `X` separated), and `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `H^q` for `q ≥ 1`. Since affine opens
134	form a basis, the presheaf homology is locally zero, and the site lemmas
135	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective` (in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`,
136	importable via `AffineSerreVanishing`) collapse the sheafification to zero. -/
137	private lemma isAffineHom_of_affine_separated (j : U ⟶ X) [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated] :
138	    IsAffineHom j := by
139	  have hg : IsSeparated (terminal.from X) := Scheme.IsSeparated.isSeparated_terminal_from
140	  have hcomp : IsAffineHom (j ≫ terminal.from X) := by
141	    have he : j ≫ terminal.from X = terminal.from U := terminal.hom_ext _ _
142	    rw [he]; infer_instance
143	  exact IsAffineHom.of_comp j (terminal.from X)
144	
145	/-- The sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor on `U`-modules, `M ↦ Γ(W, j_* M) = Γ(j⁻¹W, M)`,
146	realised as the composite `pushforward j ⋙ forget ⋙ restrictScalars 𝟙 ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ eval(W)`.
147	Its `q`-th right derived functor applied to `H` is the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
148	Project-local: a named composite so its additivity instance is synthesised once at the top
149	level (the 5-fold composite defeats in-proof instance search). -/
150	noncomputable def pushforwardSectionsFunctor (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
151	    U.Modules ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
152	  Scheme.Modules.pushforward j ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
153	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
154	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
155	    (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)
156	
157	noncomputable instance pushforwardSectionsFunctor_additive
158	    (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).Additive := by
159	  unfold pushforwardSectionsFunctor
160	  -- The flat 5-fold composite defeats instance search; build it as an explicit `instAdditiveComp`
161	  -- chain (every individual factor is additive).
162	  haveI hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j).Additive := inferInstance
163	  haveI i4 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
164	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
165	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
166	  haveI i3 : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
167	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
168	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
169	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
170	  haveI i2 : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
171	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
172	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
173	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
174	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
175	  -- Instance search will not pick up `i2` automatically here, so we pass it explicitly.
176	  exact @CategoryTheory.Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _ _ _ _ _
177	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j) hpf _ _ _ _ i2
178	
179	theorem higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
180	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
181	    (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) :
182	    IsZero (higherDirectImage j q H) := by
183	  -- `j` is an affine morphism (open immersion of an affine into a separated scheme),
184	  -- so the preimage of every affine open `V ⊆ X` is the affine open `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V`.
185	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
186	  -- Presheaf description (Stacks 01XJ): `Rᵠ j_* H ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hᵠ((j_* I•)(V)))`
187	  -- for any injective resolution `I` of `H`. Reduce to the vanishing of that sheafification.
188	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
189	    (higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology j q (injectiveResolution H))
190	  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
191	  set P := (pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H)).homology q with hP
192	  -- Reflect `IsZero` through the faithful, zero-preserving forgetful functor `toSheaf`.
193	  apply Functor.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
194	  -- Transport across the sheafification square
195	  -- `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ toPresheaf`.
196	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
197	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
198	  -- KEY VANISHING (genuine cohomological gap): the presheaf homology `Q` is a *zero object*
199	  -- over every affine open `W ⊆ X`, because `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an affine morphism) and
200	  -- `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)` for `q ≥ 1`.
201	  have hSec : ∀ (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), IsAffineOpen W →
202	      IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op W)) := by
203	    intro W hW
204	    -- Evaluate-at-`W` the presheaf complex; this functor preserves homology, so
205	    -- `Q.obj (op W) ≅ Hᵠ(Γ(W, f_* I•)) = Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`, which vanishes for affine `j⁻¹W`.
206	    set GW := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
207	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W) with hGW
208	    set Kp := pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H) with hKp
209	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GW.mapHomologyIso' (ComplexShape.up ℕ) Kp q).symm
210	    -- The section complex `n ↦ Γ(W, f_* I^n) = Γ(j⁻¹W, I^n)` is the image of the chosen
211	    -- injective resolution of `H` under the additive sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor.
212	    have hcomplex : (GW.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj Kp
213	        = ((pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
214	            (injectiveResolution H).cocomplex :=
215	      rfl
216	    rw [hcomplex]
217	    -- `isoRightDerivedObj` recognises this homology as the `q`-th right derived sections
218	    -- functor `Rᵠ Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` applied to `H`, i.e. the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
219	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
220	      (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W) q).symm
221	    -- RESIDUAL (Bridge 2): `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H) = 0` for `q ≥ 1`.  `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an
222	    -- affine morphism), so this is Serre vanishing on the affine `j⁻¹W` (`affine_serre_vanishing`
223	    -- transported across `j⁻¹W ≅ Spec Γ(j⁻¹W, 𝒪)`).
224	    sorry
225	  -- Since affine opens form a basis of `X`, every section of `Q` restricts to `0` on the
226	  -- affine opens contained in its domain, so `Q` is sectionwise locally zero and its
227	  -- sheafification vanishes.
228	  apply CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero
229	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Q := Q)
230	  intro U s
231	  refine ⟨{ arrows := fun {V} _ => ∃ W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X,
232	              IsAffineOpen W ∧ V ≤ W ∧ W ≤ U,
233	            downward_closed := ?_ }, ?_, ?_⟩
234	  · rintro V V' g ⟨A, hA, hVA, hAU⟩ h
235	    exact ⟨A, hA, le_trans (leOfHom h) hVA, hAU⟩
236	  · -- membership in the opens topology: every point of `U` has an affine basis neighbourhood
237	    intro x hx
238	    obtain ⟨W, hWmem, hxW, hWU⟩ :=
239	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp (Scheme.isBasis_affineOpens X)) hx
240	    exact ⟨W, homOfLE hWU, ⟨W, hWmem, le_refl W, hWU⟩, hxW⟩
241	  · -- the restriction of `s` to a sieve member `V ≤ A ≤ U` (with `A` affine) factors through
242	    -- `Q.obj (op A) = 0`, hence vanishes.
243	    rintro V g ⟨A, hA, hVA, hAU⟩
244	    haveI : Subsingleton (ToType (Q.obj (Opposite.op A))) :=
245	      AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSec A hA)
246	    have hgfac : g.op = (homOfLE hAU).op ≫ (homOfLE hVA).op := by
247	      rw [← op_comp]; congr 1
248	    rw [hgfac, Q.map_comp, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
249	      Subsingleton.elim ((ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map (homOfLE hAU).op)) s) 0, map_zero]
250	
251	/-- **Composition formula for higher direct images across an open immersion** (pinned
252	declaration; blueprint `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`, part (2); Stacks
253	`lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
254	
255	Let `j : U ↪ X` be an open immersion of an affine open `U` into a separated scheme `X`,
256	and `f : X ⟶ S` any morphism. For every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H` and every `k ≥ 0`:
257	```
258	  R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ R^k (f ∘ j)_* H.
259	```
260	Proof route: choose an injective resolution `H → I•` in `U.Modules`; apply `j_*`
261	degreewise to get an `f_*`-acyclic resolution `j_* I•` of `j_* H`
262	(`higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` for `j_*`-acyclicity, extended to
263	`f_*`-acyclicity via the same Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); then
264	`Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` with `G = (pushforward f)` and the identity
265	`(pushforward f) ∘ (pushforward j) = pushforward (j ≫ f)` give the stated isomorphism. -/
266	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp
267	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
268	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
269	    (f : X ⟶ S) (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (k : ℕ) :
270	    higherDirectImage f k ((pushforward j).obj H) ≅
271	      higherDirectImage (j ≫ f) k H := by
272	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
273	  -- Structural plan (all categorical building blocks below are verified to exist):
274	  --   • `G := Scheme.Modules.pushforward f` is additive and preserves finite limits
275	  --     (`inferInstance`), so `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` applies to it.
276	  --   • The pushed-forward injective resolution `K := (pushforward j).mapHomologicalComplex I•`

8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
9	
10	/-!
11	# Form-B absolute cohomology `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` (P5b input)
12	-/
13	
14	universe u
15	
16	open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian
17	
18	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
19	
20	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
21	
22	/-- The standard `HasExt` structure on `X.Modules`, made a section-local instance so the
23	`Ext`-based absolute cohomology below resolves it without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom`
24	typeclass search. -/
25	noncomputable local instance hasExtModules : HasExt.{u + 1, u, u + 1} X.Modules :=
26	  HasExt.standard _
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Form-B absolute cohomology -/
29	
30	noncomputable def jShriekOU (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules :=
31	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
32	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U))
33	
34	/-- The sheafification adjunction hom-bijection as an `AddEquiv` (additivity holds because
35	the right adjoint is an additive functor and composition is bilinear). -/
36	noncomputable def sheafificationHomAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
37	    (jShriekOU U ⟶ F) ≃+
38	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U) ⟶
39	        (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) :=
40	  { (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
41	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U)) F with
42	    map_add' := by
43	      intro f g
44	      haveI : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
45	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive := inferInstance
46	      simp only [Equiv.toFun_as_coe, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
47	      erw [Functor.map_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
48	      rfl }
49	
50	noncomputable def jShriekOU_homEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
51	    (jShriekOU U ⟶ F) ≃+
52	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
53	  (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F).trans (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv U _)
54	
55	/-- **Form-B absolute cohomology** `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{O_X}(jShriekOU U, F)`. -/
56	noncomputable def absoluteCohomology (p : ℕ) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
57	    (F : X.Modules) : AddCommGrpCat :=
58	  AddCommGrpCat.of (Ext (jShriekOU U) F p)
59	
60	/-- **`H⁰(U, F) ≅ Γ(U, F)`** as abelian groups: degree-zero `Ext` out of the
61	corepresenting object `jShriekOU U` recovers the sections of `F` over `U`. This is the
62	additive corepresentability of global sections specialised to `p = 0`. -/
63	noncomputable def absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
64	    (F : X.Modules) :
65	    Ext (jShriekOU U) F 0 ≃+
66	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
67	  (AddEquiv.mk' Ext.homEquiv₀ (by
68	    intro a b
69	    refine Ext.homEquiv₀.symm.injective ?_
70	    simp only [Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Ext.mk₀_add, Ext.mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply])).trans
71	    (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F)
72	
73	/-- **Injective vanishing**: positive-degree absolute cohomology of an injective sheaf of
74	modules vanishes. Direct from `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (the injective object is the
75	*second* `Ext` argument, so no restriction of the coefficient sheaf is needed). -/
76	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (n : ℕ) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
77	    (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) I (n + 1)) : e = 0 :=
78	  Ext.eq_zero_of_injective e
79	
80	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, surjectivity at `H^{n₁}(U, F₁)`.**
81	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` at fixed first argument
82	`jShriekOU U`, for a short exact sequence of `O_X`-modules. -/
83	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₁ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
84	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n₁ : ℕ}
85	    (x₁ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ n₁) (hx₁ : x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n₁) = 0)
86	    {n₀ : ℕ} (hn₀ : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
87	    ∃ x₃ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₃ n₀, x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₀ = x₁ :=
88	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ (jShriekOU U) hS x₁ hx₁ hn₀
89	
90	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at `H^n(U, F₂)`.**
91	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂` at fixed first argument
92	`jShriekOU U`. -/
93	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₂ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
94	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n : ℕ}
95	    (x₂ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ n) (hx₂ : x₂.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n) = 0) :
96	    ∃ x₁ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ n, x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n) = x₂ :=
97	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ (jShriekOU U) hS x₂ hx₂
98	
99	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at the connecting map.**
100	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃` at fixed first argument
101	`jShriekOU U`. -/
102	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₃ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
103	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n₀ : ℕ}
104	    (x₃ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₃ n₀) {n₁ : ℕ} (hn₁ : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
105	    (hx₃ : x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₁ = 0) :
106	    ∃ x₂ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ n₀, x₂.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n₀) = x₃ :=
107	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ (jShriekOU U) hS x₃ hn₁ hx₃
108	
109	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — naturality of `H⁰ ≅ Γ` -/
110	
111	/-- **Naturality of `Ext.homEquiv₀` in the second argument.** The degree-zero `Ext`
112	comparison intertwines composition with the degree-zero Ext class `Ext.mk₀ g` and ordinary
113	post-composition with `g`. Project-local: Mathlib has the comparison `Ext.homEquiv₀` and the
114	composite formula `Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀`, but not this intertwining as a packaged lemma. -/
115	private lemma homEquiv₀_comp_mk₀ {A B Z : X.Modules} (e : Ext A B 0) (g : B ⟶ Z) :
116	    Ext.homEquiv₀ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0)) = Ext.homEquiv₀ e ≫ g := by
117	  conv_lhs => rw [← Ext.mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply e, Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀]
118	  rw [← Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
119	
120	/-- **Naturality of `freeYonedaHomEquiv` in the coefficient presheaf.** Post-composition by a
121	morphism of presheaves of modules `q` corresponds, under the free–Yoneda evaluation bijection,
122	to applying `q` on sections over `V`. Project-local: the underlying bijection is project-local
123	(`freeYonedaHomEquiv`), so is its naturality. -/
124	private lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
125	    {F₁ F₂ : X.PresheafOfModules}
126	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F₁) (q : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
127	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F₂ (ψ ≫ q)
128	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (q.app (Opposite.op V))) (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F₁ ψ) := by
129	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app,
130	    ModuleCat.comp_apply]
131	
132	/-- **Naturality of `sheafificationHomAddEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf.** This is the
133	naturality of the sheafification adjunction's hom-bijection in its right (codomain) argument;
134	the right adjoint is `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X` (definitionally the forget–restrict
135	composite). Project-local because `sheafificationHomAddEquiv` is the project's additive
136	packaging of that bijection. -/
137	private lemma sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
138	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
139	    sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)
140	      = sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
141	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
142	
143	/-- **Naturality of `jShriekOU_homEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf.** Composing a map
144	`jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁` with `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂` corresponds, under corepresentability, to applying `g` on
145	sections over `U`. Project-local: assembled from the naturality of the two project-local halves
146	(`sheafificationHomAddEquiv` and `freeYonedaHomEquiv`). -/
147	private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
148	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
149	    jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)
150	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (Opposite.op U)))
151	          (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₁ h) := by
152	  change freeYonedaHomEquiv U _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
153	  rw [sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality, freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality]
154	  rfl
155	
156	/-- **Naturality of `H⁰(U,-) ≅ Γ(U,-)` in the coefficient sheaf**
157	(blueprint `lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural`). For `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂` in `X.Modules`, the
158	functorial action of `g` on `Ext⁰(jShriekOU U, -)` (composition with the degree-zero Ext class
159	`Ext.mk₀ g`) corresponds under the `H⁰ ≅ Γ` isomorphism to the sections map `g_U` over `U`.
160	In particular, when `g_U` is surjective so is `H⁰(U, g)` — the transfer used by the
161	surjectivity step of `lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing`. -/
162	theorem absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
163	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F₁ 0) :
164	    absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F₂ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0))
165	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (Opposite.op U)))
166	          (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F₁ e) := by
167	  change jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₂ (Ext.homEquiv₀ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0))) = _
168	  rw [homEquiv₀_comp_mk₀, jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality]
169	  rfl
170	
171	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImage
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
9	
10	/-!
11	# Presheaf description of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (Stacks 01XJ)
12	
13	This file builds project-local infrastructure towards
14	`lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`: the higher direct image `Rⁱ f_* F` is the
15	sheafification of the presheaf `V ↦ Hⁱ(f⁻¹(V), F)`.
16	
17	The categorical heart of Stacks 01XJ is that **cohomology of a complex of sheaves
18	of modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology**:
19	this holds because the sheafification functor of presheaves of modules is exact
20	(it preserves finite limits — `Mathlib`'s
21	`PresheafOfModules.instPreservesFiniteLimitsSheafification` — and, being a left
22	adjoint, finite colimits), hence preserves homology, and the counit of the
23	sheafification adjunction is an isomorphism on sheaves.
24	
25	This file isolates that engine (`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`) and feeds
26	it through the right-derived-functor computation
27	(`InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`) to express `Rⁿ f_* G`, for a chosen
28	injective resolution of `G`, as the sheafification of the presheaf-level homology
29	of the pushed-forward resolution
30	(`AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology`). The
31	remaining step to the blueprint statement — identifying that presheaf-level
32	homology with the cohomology presheaf `V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` — is handed off (see
33	the `## Why I stopped` note in the task result).
34	
35	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`
36	(`lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`, `def:higher_direct_image`).
37	-/
38	
39	universe v u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits
42	
43	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — homology of a complex under a functor -/
44	
45	namespace CategoryTheory.Functor
46	
47	/-- The homological-complex level of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`: an additive,
48	homology-preserving functor `F` commutes with taking homology of a complex,
49	`((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).homology i ≅ F.obj (K.homology i)`.
50	
51	Project-local: `Mathlib` provides this isomorphism only at the `ShortComplex`
52	level (`ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`); we lift it degreewise to
53	`HomologicalComplex` (the two `homology` objects are definitionally the homology
54	of the relevant short complexes, so the lift is the `ShortComplex` isomorphism
55	applied to `K.sc i`). -/
56	noncomputable def mapHomologyIso'
57	    {A B : Type*} [Category A] [Category B] [Abelian A] [Abelian B]
58	    (F : A ⥤ B) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]

60	    ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).homology i ≅ F.obj (K.homology i) :=

65	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheaf homology is sheafified presheaf homology -/
66	
67	namespace PresheafOfModules
68	
69	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
70	  {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{v}} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v}} (α : R₀ ⟶ R.obj)
71	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
72	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
73	  {ιc : Type} (cc : ComplexShape ιc)

76	binary products, being a left adjoint that preserves finite limits).
77

79	complexes via `mapHomologicalComplex`. -/
80	noncomputable instance sheafificationAdditive :
81	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).Additive :=
82	  Functor.additive_of_preserves_binary_products _
83	
84	/-- The counit of the sheafification adjunction, applied levelwise to a complex of
85	sheaves of modules, is an isomorphism of complexes
86	`sheafify (forget K) ≅ K`. This is the sheaf-side input to
87	`homologyIsoSheafify`: a sheaf complex is recovered (up to canonical iso) by
88	sheafifying its underlying presheaf complex.
89	
90	Project-local: assembled from `Mathlib`'s `IsIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit`
91	via `isoOfComponents`. -/
92	noncomputable def counitComplexIso (K : HomologicalComplex (SheafOfModules.{v} R) cc) :
93	    ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
94	        (((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
95	          cc).obj K) ≅ K :=
96	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents
97	    (fun n => (asIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit).app (K.X n))
98	    (fun n m _ => ((sheafificationAdjunction α).counit.naturality (K.d n m)).symm)
99	
100	/-- **Engine for Stacks 01XJ.** Homology of a complex of sheaves of modules is the
101	sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of its underlying
102	presheaf complex:
103	`K.homology i ≅ (sheafification α).obj ((forget K).homology i)`.
104	
105	The proof transports homology across the counit isomorphism `sheafify (forget K) ≅ K`
106	of `counitComplexIso` and then uses that `sheafification α` preserves homology
107	(it is exact: preserves finite limits and, as a left adjoint, finite colimits),
108	via `Functor.mapHomologyIso'`.
109	
110	Project-local: `Mathlib` has the exactness ingredients but not this packaged
111	"cohomology sheaf = sheafify of objectwise cohomology" comparison. -/
112	noncomputable def homologyIsoSheafify (K : HomologicalComplex (SheafOfModules.{v} R) cc)
113	    (i : ιc) :
114	    K.homology i ≅ (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).obj
115	      ((((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
116	        cc).obj K).homology i) :=
117	  (HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor _ cc i).mapIso (counitComplexIso α cc K).symm ≪≫
118	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).mapHomologyIso' cc
119	      (((SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex
120	        cc).obj K) i
121	
122	end PresheafOfModules

124	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Rⁿ f_*` as a sheafified presheaf homology -/
125	
126	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
127	
128	variable {S X : Scheme.{u}}
129	
130	/-- The underlying presheaf-of-modules complex of the pushed-forward injective
131	resolution `f_* I^•`, whose `n`-th objectwise homology is the cohomology presheaf
132	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` (with the implicit choice of injective resolution `I`).
133	
134	Project-local: the presheaf whose sheafification is `Rⁿ f_* G`. -/
135	noncomputable def pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex
136	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] (f : X ⟶ S) {G : X.Modules}
137	    (I : InjectiveResolution G) :
138	    HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf.obj) (ComplexShape.up ℕ) :=
139	  ((SheafOfModules.forget S.ringCatSheaf ⋙
140	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapHomologicalComplex
141	        (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
142	    (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj I.cocomplex)
143	
144	/-- **Stacks 01XJ, resolution form.** For any chosen injective resolution
145	`I : InjectiveResolution G`, the `n`-th higher direct image `Rⁿ f_* G` is the
146	sheafification of the presheaf-level homology of the pushed-forward resolution
147	`f_* I^•`. Concretely, `Rⁿ f_* G ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hⁿ((f_* I^•)(V)))`, and the
148	presheaf `V ↦ Hⁿ((f_* I^•)(V)) = Hⁿ(I^•(f⁻¹ V))` is the cohomology presheaf
149	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` of the blueprint statement.
150	
151	The proof composes `InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj` (computing the right
152	derived functor `Rⁿ f_* = (f_*).rightDerived n` via the resolution) with the
153	sheafification engine `PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`.
154	
155	Project-local: the resolution-dependent form of `lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf`;
156	the identification of the displayed presheaf with the absolute cohomology presheaf
157	`V ↦ Hⁿ(f⁻¹(V), G)` is the remaining hand-off step. -/
158	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology
159	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] (f : X ⟶ S) (n : ℕ) {G : X.Modules}
160	    (I : InjectiveResolution G) :
161	    higherDirectImage f n G ≅
162	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
163	        ((pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex f I).homology n) :=
164	  I.isoRightDerivedObj (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f) n ≪≫
165	    PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify (𝟙 S.ringCatSheaf.obj) (ComplexShape.up ℕ)
166	      (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
167	        I.cocomplex) n
168	
169	end AlgebraicGeometry

306	/-- A **covering datum** on `X`: an index family of opens (the covered open is `⨆ i, c.2 i`).
307	Project-local: the lightweight cover representation matching the cover-local Čech lemmas
308	(`cechCohomology`, `faceShortComplex`), which are indexed by a family `ι → Opens X`. -/
309	abbrev CovDatum (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type (u + 1) :=
310	  Σ ι : Type u, ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X
311	
312	/-- **Cover system on a basis** (Stacks 01EO conditions (1)–(2)). Records a basis `B`, a set of
313	admissible coverings `Cov`, the faces-in-basis datum (every finite intersection of a covering's
314	opens lies in `B`, condition (1)), and the cofinality datum in the form it is consumed —
315	condition (2) packaged as the section-surjectivity it produces through `ses_cech_h1`: for every
316	short exact sequence `S` of `O_X`-modules whose left term has vanishing higher Čech cohomology,
317	the section map of `S.g` is surjective over every basic open. The auxiliary `injective_acyclic`
318	field records the standard-cover injective Čech-acyclicity (Stacks
319	`lemma-injective-trivial-cech`, i.e. `injective_cech_acyclic`) for the system's coverings.
320	Project-local: bespoke encoding; the two sheaf-theoretic fields are discharged at the affine
321	(02KG) instantiation by `ses_cech_h1` + standard-cover cofinality and by `injective_cech_acyclic`
322	respectively. -/
323	structure BasisCovSystem (X : Scheme.{u}) where
324	  /-- The basis of opens. -/
325	  B : Set (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
326	  /-- The set of admissible coverings. -/
327	  Cov : Set (CovDatum X)
328	  /-- Condition (1): every finite intersection of a covering's opens lies in the basis. -/
329	  faces_mem : ∀ c ∈ Cov, ∀ (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → c.1), (⨅ k, c.2 (σ k)) ∈ B
330	  /-- Condition (2), in the section-surjectivity shape produced by `ses_cech_h1` + cofinality. -/
331	  surj_of_vanishing : ∀ (S : ShortComplex X.Modules), S.ShortExact →
332	    (∀ c ∈ Cov, ∀ q, 0 < q →
333	      IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj S.X₁) q)) →
334	    ∀ V ∈ B, Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
335	      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
336	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map S.g)).app (Opposite.op V)))
337	  /-- Injective Čech-acyclicity for the system's coverings (`injective_cech_acyclic`). -/
338	  injective_acyclic : ∀ (I : X.Modules), Injective I → ∀ c ∈ Cov, ∀ q, 0 < q →
339	    IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj I) q)

342	`O_X`-module `F` satisfies this when `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, F) = 0` for every covering `𝒰 ∈ s.Cov` and every

344	induction feeds the quotient `Q = I/F` back as coefficient, and `Q` need not be quasi-coherent.
345	Project-local: the inductive predicate of the 01EO argument. -/
346	@[reducible] def HasVanishingHigherCech (s : BasisCovSystem X) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
347	  ∀ c ∈ s.Cov, ∀ p, 0 < p →
348	    IsZero (cechCohomology c.2 ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) p)

351	
352	-- The dimension-shift induction embeds modules into injectives, which needs

357	
358	/-- The injective-embedding short exact sequence `0 → F → I → I/F → 0` of an `O_X`-module `F`,
359	with `I = Injective.under F` the chosen injective and `I/F` its cokernel. Project-local helper for
360	the 01EO dimension-shift induction; carries `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis. -/
361	@[reducible] private noncomputable def injSES [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (F : X.Modules) :
362	    ShortComplex X.Modules :=
363	  ShortComplex.mk (Injective.ι F) (cokernel.π (Injective.ι F)) (cokernel.condition _)

365	private lemma injSES_shortExact [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (F : X.Modules) :
366	    (injSES F).ShortExact :=

370	`s` on a basis and any `O_X`-module `F` with vanishing higher Čech cohomology for `s`, the
371	absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(U, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ∈ s.B` and
372	`p > 0`. Induction on `p`, quantified over all `F` in the inductive class: the quotient
373	`Q = I/F` re-enters the class (via the section Čech SES of L1 from the per-face SES, plus L2
374	quotient-vanishing and the cover system's injective acyclicity), the base case is L3, and the
375	inductive step is the covariant Ext long exact sequence. Project-local: the 01EO inductive core. -/
376	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
377	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F)
378	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X} (hU : U ∈ s.B) {p : ℕ} (hp : 0 < p)

381	      ∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), U ∈ s.B →
382	        ∀ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F (n + 1), e = 0 := by
383	    intro n
384	    induction n with
385	    | zero =>
386	      intro F hF U hU e
387	      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
388	      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X₂ := inferInstance
389	      exact absoluteCohomology_one_eq_zero_of_basis U hS
390	        (s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF U hU) e
391	    | succ m ih =>
392	      intro F hF U hU e
393	      have hS := injSES_shortExact F
394	      haveI : Injective (injSES F).X₂ := inferInstance
395	      -- The quotient `Q = (injSES F).X₃` again has vanishing higher Čech cohomology.
396	      have hQ : HasVanishingHigherCech s (injSES F).X₃ := by
397	        intro c hc q hq
398	        set P := (injSES F).map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X) with hP
399	        have hface : ∀ (pp : ℕ) (σ : Fin (pp + 1) → c.1),
400	            (faceShortComplex c.2 P σ).ShortExact := by
401	          intro pp σ
402	          refine faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses c.2 (injSES F) hS ?_ pp σ
403	          intro pp' σ'
404	          exact s.surj_of_vanishing (injSES F) hS hF _ (s.faces_mem c hc pp' σ')
405	        have hSES := cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis c.2 P hface
406	        exact quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis c.2 P hSES
407	          (s.injective_acyclic (injSES F).X₂ inferInstance c hc) (hF c hc) q hq
408	      -- Ext long exact sequence step: `H^{m+2}(F)` is squeezed by `H^{m+1}(Q) = 0` (IH) and
409	      -- `H^{m+2}(I) = 0` (injective vanishing).
410	      have hef : e.comp (Ext.mk₀ (injSES F).f) (add_zero (m + 1 + 1)) = 0 :=
411	        absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (m + 1) U (injSES F).X₂ _
412	      obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
413	        absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₁ U hS e hef (rfl : (m + 1) + 1 = m + 1 + 1)

417	  exact key n F hF U hU e

420	
421	/-- **Čech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (Stacks 01EO).** Let `s` be a cover system on a
422	basis for `X` and `F` an `O_X`-module with vanishing higher Čech cohomology for `s` (conditions
423	(1)–(3) of 01EO, packaged in `s` and `hF`). Then the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(U, F) =
424	Extᵖ(jShriekOU U, F)` vanishes for every basic open `U ∈ s.B` and every `p > 0`. The thin assembly
425	of the dimension-shift induction `absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis` (L4), which itself chains
426	the section-Čech short exact sequence (L1), quotient vanishing (L2), and the base case (L3).
427	Project-local: the named 01EO target, carrying `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` as a hypothesis (the
428	instance is absent in Mathlib for sheaves of modules; see `injSES`). -/
429	theorem cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (s : BasisCovSystem X)
430	    {F : X.Modules} (hF : HasVanishingHigherCech s F) :
431	    ∀ U ∈ s.B, ∀ p, 0 < p → ∀ e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F p, e = 0 :=
432	  fun _ hU _ hp e => absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_basis s hF hU hp e

396	/-- **A standard cover of `D(f)` spans the unit ideal of `R_f`.** If the distinguished opens
397	`D(gᵢ)` cover `D(f)` in `Spec R`, then the images `gᵢ ↦ R_f` of the cover family span the whole
398	ring `R_f = Localization.Away f`: pulled back along `Spec R_f ≅ D(f) ↪ Spec R` the union of the
399	`D(gᵢ)` becomes `D(f)` itself, which is all of `Spec R_f` since `f` is a unit there. Project-local:
400	the spanning hypothesis required to apply the standard-cover tilde {\v C}ech vanishing
401	`sectionCech_affine_vanishing` over `R_f` in the quasi-coherent seed's change-of-base leaf. -/
402	theorem affine_cover_span_localizationAway {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} [Finite ι]

405	      = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i)) :
406	    Ideal.span (Set.range (fun i => algebraMap R (Localization.Away f) (g i))) = ⊤ := by

408	  simp only [← PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen]
409	  rw [← map_iSup, ← hcov, PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen, eq_top_iff]
410	  rintro p -
411	  rw [PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen]
412	  exact fun hmem => p.isPrime.ne_top

The genuine blocker is becoming clear. Before concluding, let me test whether the foundational functor identity `pushforward j ⋙ sectionsFunctor W ≅ sectionsFunctor (j⁻¹W)` is tractable, and understand the pushforward sections behavior. Let me write a scratch test.

input.code:
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AffineSerreVanishing
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImagePresheaf
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AcyclicResolution

universe u
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry Scheme.Modules

variable {U X : Scheme.{u}}

-- What is `pushforward j ⋙ sectionsFunctor W` vs `sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`?
example (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    Scheme.Modules.pushforward j ⋙ AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor W = 
      AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W) := by
  rfl

content: {"success":true,"diagnostics":[]}

`pushforward j ⋙ sectionsFunctor W = sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)` holds by `rfl`. Let me verify `pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W = sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)` and check for existing bridges between `sectionsFunctor.rightDerived` and `Ext (jShriekOU)`.

input.code:
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AffineSerreVanishing
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImagePresheaf
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AcyclicResolution
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.OpenImmersionPushforward

universe u
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry Scheme.Modules

variable {U X : Scheme.{u}}

example (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W = 
      AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W) := by
  rfl

content: {"success":true,"diagnostics":[]}

Excellent — both functor identities hold by `rfl`. The leaf reduces to `IsZero (((sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)).rightDerived q).obj H)`. Now I need bridge (a): connecting `(sectionsFunctor V).rightDerived q` to `Ext (jShriekOU V)`. Let me verify the deepbridge-2b Mathlib lemma signatures exist.

private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :

    ModuleCat.comp_apply]

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AffineSerreVanishing
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImagePresheaf
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AcyclicResolution
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.OpenImmersionPushforward

universe u
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry Scheme.Modules Opposite

namespace AlgebraicGeometry
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
    jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (op V)))
          (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁ h) := by
  have hs : sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
        = sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
  change freeYonedaHomEquiv V _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
  rw [hs]
  change freeYonedaHomEquiv V _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₁ h ≫ _) = _
  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.comp_apply]
  rfl

end AlgebraicGeometry

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AffineSerreVanishing
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.HigherDirectImagePresheaf
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AcyclicResolution
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.OpenImmersionPushforward

universe u
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry Scheme.Modules Opposite

namespace AlgebraicGeometry
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
    jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (op V)))
          (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁ h) := by
  have hs : sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
        = sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
        (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
  change freeYonedaHomEquiv V _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
  rw [hs, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.comp_apply]
  rfl

/-- The sections functor `Γ(V,-)` is corepresented by `jShriekOU V`. -/
noncomputable def sectionsFunctorCorepIso (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V)) :=
  NatIso.ofComponents
    (fun F => (AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F)).symm)
    (by
      intro F₁ F₂ g
      ext s
      exact (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂).injective (by
        simp only [AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso]
        sorry))

end AlgebraicGeometry

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules Opposite

variable {U X S : Scheme.{u}}

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — corepresentability of the sections functor

The sections functor `Γ(V, -)` on `X.Modules` is corepresented by the Form-B object
`jShriekOU V`.  Consequently its right derived functors agree, degreewise, with those of the
Hom-functor `Hom(jShriekOU V, -)` — the homological half of the identification
`Rᵠ Γ(V, -) ≅ Ext^q(jShriekOU V, -)` (the deep "Bridge (1)/(2)" connecting the higher-direct-image
presheaf homology to absolute cohomology). -/

/-- **Naturality of `jShriekOU_homEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf** (reproduced here; the version
in `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` is `private`). Composing a map `jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁` with `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂`
corresponds, under corepresentability, to applying `g` on sections over `V`. -/
private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
    jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (op V)))
          (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁ h) := by
  have hs : sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
        = sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
        (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
  change freeYonedaHomEquiv V _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
  rw [hs, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.comp_apply]
  rfl

/-- `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X` is additive (it is the additive composite
`forget ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙)`). Project-local instance so `sectionsFunctor` is additive. -/
noncomputable instance toPresheafOfModules_additive :
    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).Additive :=
  inferInstanceAs (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive

/-- The sections functor `Γ(V,-) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab` is additive. Project-local instance (the flat
triple composite does not infer additivity automatically). -/
noncomputable instance sectionsFunctor_additive (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    (AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).Additive := by
  unfold AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor
  haveI i2 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (op V)).Additive :=
    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
  exact Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _

/-- **The sections functor `Γ(V,-)` is corepresented by `jShriekOU V`.** A natural isomorphism
`sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V))`, assembled from the additive
corepresentability bijection `jShriekOU_homEquiv` and its naturality. Project-local: the functorial
upgrade of `jShriekOU_homEquiv`, the geometric input to the absolute-cohomology bridge. -/
noncomputable def sectionsFunctorCorepIso (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V)) :=
  NatIso.ofComponents
    (fun F => (AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F)).symm)
    (by
      intro F₁ F₂ g
      ext s
      simp only [AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso, comp_apply, Iso.symm_hom, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
        AddEquiv.coe_toAddMonoidHom]
      rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_eq]
      change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).map g)) s
        = jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s ≫ g)
      rw [jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s) g, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
      rfl)

/-- **Right derived functors transport along a natural isomorphism of additive functors.**
Project-local: assembled from `NatTrans.rightDerived` of `τ.hom`/`τ.inv` and functoriality
(`NatTrans.rightDerived_comp`, `NatTrans.rightDerived_id`). -/
noncomputable def rightDerivedNatIso {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Abelian C]
    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [Abelian D] {F G : C ⥤ D} [F.Additive] [G.Additive]
    (τ : F ≅ G) (n : ℕ) : F.rightDerived n ≅ G.rightDerived n where
  hom := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.hom n
  inv := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.inv n
  hom_inv_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.hom_inv_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]
  inv_hom_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.inv_hom_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]
